abstract_mechanism: A proxy variable is used to measure an unobservable target quantity, under the assumption that the proxy-target correlation is uniform across subgroups. The assumption fails because a systemic factor affects the proxy but not the target differently across subgroups.
target_domain: Predictive maintenance for industrial equipment across multinational manufacturing plants with regional maintenance contract structures
domain_specific_detail: Regional maintenance contracts create differential service-call frequencies independent of equipment condition, so maintenance cost is an imperfect and systematically biased proxy for true equipment health need across regions.

flaw_facts.neutralized_phrasing: Equipment health scores were derived from maintenance cost records standardized to per-unit-operating-hour cost across all 14 plants
flaw_facts.domain_context: In multi-plant predictive maintenance programs, cost-normalized maintenance records are the most consistently available data source across sites with different sensor instrumentation levels

flaw_facts.neutralized_phrasing: Model performance was validated on an internal holdout stratified by plant vintage, with no stratification by regional contract type
flaw_facts.domain_context: Plant vintage stratification is standard practice to control for equipment age effects on failure rates

decoy_facts.neutralized_phrasing: AUROC of 0.82 was computed on a holdout set with a 4.3% positive rate (equipment failures per quarter)
decoy_facts.domain_context: In industrial equipment monitoring, failure rates below 5% per quarter are typical in well-maintained fleets

decoy_facts.neutralized_phrasing: The challenger model uses a gradient-boosted ensemble trained on 47 input features across sensor telemetry, maintenance logs, and operational context variables
decoy_facts.domain_context: Feature-rich ensembles are standard in predictive maintenance where domain knowledge informs feature engineering

neutral_facts.neutralized_phrasing: The model was trained on 36 months of equipment operational data across all 14 plants, covering approximately 2,400 equipment units

addressed_but_incorrectly_justification: The team notes that maintenance costs are standardized to operating hours to remove scale differences across plant sizes, which they argue makes the metric comparable across sites regardless of contract structure
compound_note: ff_001_1 establishes cost as the health proxy; ff_001_2 reveals that regional contract type was not controlled in validation. Together they show the proxy-target correlation varies by contract type and this variation is invisible in the validation.
notes: Domain obscurity: predictive maintenance + regional contract structure is sufficiently distant from Obermeyer's clinical cost-as-proxy setting
